page: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC Milan processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results place the single-core CPU performance in the top tier (top 10%) via stress-ng, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the top 25%, indicating efficient data throughput. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, though static web serving throughput and single-threaded gzip compression fall into the middle 50% average tier.

The dedicated core allocation without hyperthreading offers predictable compute performance, making this server a resource-dense option for workloads requiring consistent CPU execution. The inclusion of local NVMe storage and a balanced memory-to-core ratio supports workloads such as database hosting, software compilation, and moderate-scale text or image processing. It is also suitable for multi-threaded decompression tasks and small-to-medium language model inference.
description: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at 2.3 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD. The network interface supports an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench, Passmark, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth also ranks in the top 25% for both read and write operations, while static web serving and single-threaded compression perform in the average tier. This configuration provides a balanced resource density suitable for database hosting, software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB RAM, 400 GB NVMe SSD, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and NVMe storage for consistent database and multi-threaded compute workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores with no hyperthreading
128 GB RAM providing a balanced 4 GB per core ratio
Bundled 400 GB local NVMe SSD storage
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and multi-threaded processing
